title: I'm drowning a bit — April 4 journal entry (Rankett treadmill, Emily ending, dad as cook)
html:
TL;DR

  • A raw journal entry — "I feel like im drowning a bit. Where the water is starting to come up to my mouth" — written six weeks after the Feb 21 doubt spiral and one month after the cons list.
  • Names six simultaneous pressures: Rankett is a treadmill making no progress; the Emily relationship "seems to probably be coming to a close"; mom is aging; baby brother is far; he's in Austin for "an abstract ideal, with none of MY people"; can barely afford rent without dad's help.
  • A new and uncomfortable line about his dad: "My dad is a cook, he lies to me about opportunities he is approached and it's fucked because i sometimes do the same." — first explicit father-mirroring in the vault.
  • The Austin loneliness thread surfaces by name: "I dont have any friends in austin… Charlie kinda, Cheese kinda, Hec kinda."
  • Counterfactual escape vector: "lets assume that I moved back to oregon portland?" — names Owen, Huck, Luke, Ayden as the boys he'd be near, but acknowledges he "still wouldn't be with Kyle, Cam, Emilio, Edgar."

The texture of the entry

The doc closes with the same self-talk pattern that recurs across the Yahir talks to himself archive:

"Continue Yahir, this is you talking. Continue. Push even harder… Don't waver for your mission. Visualize your life and who you want to become. It's important to breathe. You must."

Same structural move as 2026 02 21 thoughts and things 2 21 2026 ("You have yourself, you have your spirit. You have God's eyes. You have your dad and your mom") and the April 14 'God's eyes' entry. The breath-prompt is becoming load-bearing.

What's actually new vs. prior entries

  • Father-as-cook line — first time Yahir explicitly admits he also lies about opportunities, mirroring his dad's pattern. Self-recognition of inherited behavior, not just observation.
  • Affordability admission — "only reason im even able to scrap by is because my dad is generous enough to help me out" — explicit financial dependency on dad while simultaneously critiquing him. New layer to family as runway.
  • Three Austin friends named with hedge — Charlie, Cheese, Hec all "kinda". Each gets a fractional credit, none gets a full one.
  • Couples-anxiety — "Im no fucking idol… i feel a bit scared of the couples. Who will die, type of anxiety…" — death-of-relationship dread inflected with literal mortality dread.

Privacy notes

  • Sheri mentioned in passing ("a picture of my mom that sheri sent") — likely a relative; first-name only, single mention. Queue to Potential Entities.
  • Charlie / Cheese / Hec — first-name-only Austin acquaintances, single-mention each. Queue.
  • Luke — fourth Oregon-boys name (after Owen / Huck / Ayden); first-name only. Single mention; queue.
